Details: Membrane filtration alone is generally not very effective for the removal of uranium (less than 60 percent depending on the membrane type and pH).  One study found 0.45 micron membrane filtration to remove 50 to 60 percent of uranium between pHs 6.5 and 9.  The article suggested that the hydrolyzed uranyl complexes were polymerized and thus were retained on the membrane.  Typically, however, membrane filtration followed coagulation/flocculation which were suspected to be responsible for most of the uranium removal.
